question: In one or two sentences, what is this dataset?
summary: Provides a detailed description of the Element.
description:
This field is a detailed description of the Element. It may also be extracted
from the Element itself.

The intent is to provide recipients of the SPDX file with a detailed technical
explanation of the functionality, anticipated use, and anticipated
implementation of the Element.

This field may also include a description of improvements over prior versions
of the Element.

retrieval.hypothetical_passage:
# Dataset Card for SQuAD v2

SQuAD 2.0 combines the 100,000 questions in SQuAD 1.1 with over 50,000 unanswerable questions written adversarially by crowdworkers to look similar to answerable ones. Each example contains a Wikipedia paragraph, a natural-language question, and span-level answer annotations. The dataset is widely used as a reading-comprehension benchmark for transformer-based QA models. Released alongside the EMNLP 2018 paper by Rajpurkar et al.

extraction.instruction: Extract a one-to-two-sentence factual description of the dataset, summarising what it contains, how it was built, and its intended task.
extraction.field_spec: SPDX property `description` (Core profile). Free-form xsd:string, plain text. Format: one or two complete sentences, no markdown, no headings, no trailing newline.
extraction.output_guidance: Prefer the first paragraph of the dataset card's summary or the paper abstract's opening sentence. If multiple sources conflict, prefer the canonical HuggingFace dataset card over a third-party README. Strip markdown, citations, and badge syntax. If no descriptive text appears in the sources, return noAssertion rather than synthesising a description from the dataset name.
